subject: fix(deploy): add GitHub Pages deployment workflow and fix base paths
body:
GitHub Pages was not deploying because:
1. No GitHub Actions workflow existed to build and deploy
2. Vite had no `base` config — assets loaded from `/` instead of `/four-ems/`
3. No 404.html for SPA routing — React Router deep links returned 404
4. BrowserRouter had no basename — routes didn't match under /four-ems/
5. PWA manifest scope/start_url pointed to `/` instead of `/four-ems/`
6. Embed code dialog used `window.location.origin` without base path

Added:
- .github/workflows/deploy.yml — builds on push to main, deploys via
  actions/deploy-pages (artifact-based, GitHub's recommended approach)
- public/404.html — redirects to index.html preserving path for SPA routing
- index.html restore script — converts 404 redirect query back to real path

Fixed:
- vite.config.ts: added `base: '/four-ems/'` and updated PWA manifest paths
- src/main.tsx: added `basename="/four-ems"` to BrowserRouter
- BuilderPage.tsx: embed baseUrl now includes BASE_URL from Vite

Tags: deployment, github-pages, pwa, routing, ci
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 4
Impact: infrastructure
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: deployment
Semver: minor

title: add GitHub Pages deployment workflow and fix base paths
